deeper_desc_trigger_gaze_target: Fires when the user's gaze lands inside the rect for at least min_dwell_ms. Pick a region of the video frame, then choose what should happen.
deeper_desc_trigger_gaze_avoid: Fires when the user's gaze stays OUTSIDE the rect for at least min_dwell_ms. Useful for nudging attention back to where you want it.
deeper_desc_trigger_attention_lost: Fires once when the user's face leaves the camera for at least min_duration_ms and then returns. Use this to pull attention back if they look away.
deeper_desc_trigger_blink_detected: Fires on every detected blink. Best paired with cooldowns so back-to-back blinks don't stack effects.
deeper_desc_trigger_mouth_open: Fires when the user opens their mouth. Combine with a region constraint if you want it only during specific moments.
deeper_desc_trigger_time_reached: Fires once when playback reaches the given time. The simplest trigger; use these to script a session.
deeper_desc_trigger_region_entered: Fires once each time the playhead enters the named region. Pair with region_exited for symmetric setup/teardown.
deeper_desc_trigger_region_exited: Fires once each time the playhead leaves the named region.
deeper_desc_action_seek: Jumps playback to a target time, or to the start/end of a named region.
deeper_desc_action_loop_region: Seeks back to the start of the current region (or a named one). Combine with a time_reached trigger at the region's end to make it loop.
deeper_desc_action_pause: Pauses media playback. The user has to resume manually.
deeper_desc_action_play_audio: Plays a short audio cue. Path is absolute or relative to the assets folder; ducks other audio by default.
deeper_desc_action_trigger_haptic: Sends a haptic pattern to the connected device. Pick a stock pattern or design a custom curve.
deeper_desc_action_screen_shake: Brief camera-shake of every visible window. Higher intensity = bigger jitter; duration controls how long it lasts.
deeper_desc_action_trigger_effect: Fires a CCP effect (haptic, flash, bubble, subliminal text, or screen overlay) with its own settings. Useful for 'when this trigger happens, do that effect' wiring.
deeper_editor_rule_band_drag_hint: Drag the band on the timeline to adjust start / end.

deeper_editor_help_region_body:
Region constraint restricts a rule so it only fires while the playhead is inside a specific region (a band on the timeline).

• Set to "(any time)" — the rule can fire at any point in the media.
• Set to a region — the rule is dormant until playback enters that region, and goes back to dormant when playback leaves it.

Use it to scope behaviors to a section: e.g. "only flash subliminals during the chorus," or "only react to gaze drift during the trance segment."
deeper_desc_action_set_intensity: Adjusts session intensity. (Stubbed in v1 — no central intensity setting yet.)
deeper_editor_preview_keys_hint: B=blink  M=mouth  A=attention-lost  Mouse-as-gaze inside preview

deeper_welcome_card_body:
Deeper is a creator system for adding haptics, gaze triggers, and timed actions to any audio or video. Author once, share the .ccpenh.json file with anyone running CCP.

There's a bundled demo ("Welcome to Deeper") in your library — open it to see how the pieces fit together, or start the tour for a guided walkthrough.
deeper_editor_help_tooltip: Walk through this editor again — timeline, side panel, preview mode.
deeper_editor_tt_play: Play / pause the media in the preview pane (Space).
deeper_editor_tt_preview: Preview mode runs your enhancement live so you can feel it before you save. Webcam triggers use your camera if available; otherwise B/M/A keys simulate blink/mouth/attention-lost and the mouse stands in for gaze.
deeper_editor_tt_timeline: Timeline. Click or drag to seek. Shift-drag on the top half marks a new region. Press R to mark a 5-second region at the playhead, H to drop a haptic event.
deeper_editor_tt_meta_name: Display name shown in the library and player.
deeper_editor_tt_meta_creator: Your handle. Stored in the file so anyone who plays it knows who made it.
deeper_editor_tt_meta_description: What this enhancement does and who it's for. Shown in the library tile.
deeper_editor_tt_meta_tags: Comma-separated keywords for searching ("hypno, edge, deep"). Optional.
deeper_editor_tt_meta_license: How others can use this file. CC0 = public domain, CC-BY = credit me, etc. Optional but encouraged.
deeper_editor_tt_media_source: The file or URL the enhancement is bound to. Set when you created it; * means "any media" (works in the player on whatever audio is loaded).
deeper_editor_tt_media_type: Whether this enhancement targets audio or video. Determines which triggers are available.
deeper_editor_tt_rules_section: Rules are trigger → action pairs. Triggers fire on time, region, gaze, blink, etc. Actions seek, pause, play audio, send haptics, etc. Use + to add a rule.
deeper_editor_tt_region_label: Friendly name shown on the timeline ("Deepen", "Rest", etc.).
deeper_editor_tt_region_end: Region end time in seconds. Must be greater than start.
deeper_editor_tt_region_color: Hex color for the region bar on the timeline. Click a swatch below for quick picks.
deeper_editor_tt_haptic_start: When this haptic event begins, in seconds from media start.
deeper_editor_tt_haptic_duration: How long the haptic event lasts, in seconds.
deeper_editor_tt_haptic_intensity: Master intensity from 0 to 1. The pattern shape (below) is multiplied by this value.
deeper_editor_tt_haptic_pattern: Stock pattern (Pulse, Wave, Throb, etc.) or Custom… to draw your own curve.
deeper_editor_tt_haptic_test: Send this event to your connected device right now so you can feel it.
deeper_editor_tt_rule_enabled: Disabled rules stay in the file but don't fire. Useful while iterating.
deeper_editor_tt_rule_trigger: What makes this rule fire. Time-based triggers (time_reached, region_entered/exited) work for any media. Gaze, blink, attention-lost, and mouth-open need video + a webcam.
deeper_editor_tt_rule_action: What happens when the trigger fires.
deeper_editor_tt_rule_region: Restrict this rule to fire only while the playhead is inside a specific region. "(any time)" = no restriction.
deeper_editor_tt_rule_cooldown: Minimum gap between firings, in milliseconds. Stops repeated triggers (every blink, region re-entry) from spamming.
deeper_editor_tt_save: Save the enhancement to disk (Ctrl+S). Saves to its existing path, or prompts for one if new.
deeper_editor_tt_validation: Validation runs on every change. Errors block save; warnings are advisory.
deeper_editor_tt_sidebar_splitter: Drag to resize the side panel (320–520 px). Width persists across sessions.
deeper_editor_tt_metadata_drawer: Show or hide the metadata fields (Name, Creator, Description, Tags, License).
deeper_editor_tt_lane_rules: Rules — trigger → action pairs. Right-click the timeline, or use + Rule, to add one.
deeper_editor_tt_lane_regions: Regions — coloured bands on the timeline. Press R to drop a 5-second region at the playhead.
deeper_editor_tt_lane_haptics: Haptic events — vibration pulses bound to the connected toy. Press H to drop one at the playhead.
deeper_editor_tt_lane_effects: Effects — flash, bubble, subliminal, overlay. Use + Effect or right-click the timeline.
deeper_editor_tip_add_rule_hero: Add a rule at the current playhead (right-click the timeline for more trigger types).
deeper_editor_tip_add_effect_hero: Add an effect at the current playhead (right-click the timeline for the full effect menu).

deeper_editor_tip_zoom_in: Zoom in (or hold Ctrl and scroll).

deeper_editor_tip_creator_lock: When locked, HypnoTube metadata can't overwrite the Creator field. Click to unlock and edit manually.
deeper_editor_tip_region_delete: Delete this region (does not delete rules constrained to it).
deeper_editor_tip_haptic_reset_curve: Reset the haptic curve to a flat profile at the slider intensity.
deeper_editor_tip_rule_delete: Delete this rule (and drop its companion region if no other rule references it).
deeper_editor_tip_rule_band_label: Label for the constraint region this rule fires inside.
deeper_editor_tip_rule_band_color: Hex colour of the constraint band on the timeline.
deeper_editor_tip_rule_band_delete: Delete this rule (does not delete the constraint region — use the Regions lane for that).
deeper_editor_tip_flash_duration: How long the flash stays on screen, in milliseconds.
deeper_editor_tip_bubble_window: How long the bubble burst runs, in seconds.
deeper_editor_tip_bubble_intensity: Burst rate. 0 = a trickle, 1 = a wall.
deeper_editor_tip_subliminal_text: Text to flash. Keep it short — under a second of reading.
deeper_editor_tip_subliminal_duration: How long the subliminal is visible, in milliseconds.
deeper_editor_tip_overlay_kind: Which overlay to draw — Pink Filter, Spiral, or Brain Drain.
deeper_editor_tip_overlay_duration: How long the overlay stays on screen, in milliseconds.
deeper_tut_tab_intro_body:
Deeper lets you wire haptics, gaze triggers, and timed cues into any audio or video. The file you create (.ccpenh.json) is portable — anyone running CCP can play it.

This tour takes about a minute.
deeper_tut_tab_player_body:
The player runs an enhancement against a piece of media. Pick the audio, pick the enhancement, hit play — haptics and rules fire on the timeline.

If an enhancement file sits next to your audio with the same name, it loads automatically.
deeper_tut_tab_new_body: Pick a media source (local file or URL) and a type (audio or video). The editor opens with the timeline ready to go.
deeper_tut_tab_library_body: Everything in your Deeper folder shows up here. Click any row to open it in the editor. Filter by type, tag, or search by name. The bundled "Welcome to Deeper" demo is here on first launch — open it to see a working example with regions, haptics, and rules.
deeper_tut_tab_recent_body: Last few files you opened. Includes anything outside your library too.
deeper_tut_tab_filters_body: Search by name or creator. Filter by type or tag. Sort by Recent, Name, or Creator. With a growing library this is how you find things fast.
deeper_tut_tab_export_body:
You can now bundle an enhancement directly into a copy of an MP4, MP3, or WAV. Find it in the editor under File → Export as Media… (Ctrl+E), or the button next to Save.

The exported file plays normally in any player — and CCP loads the effects automatically when you open it back in the Deeper player. No more sidecar .ccpenh.json files: hand someone the media and they get the enhancement for free.
deeper_tut_tab_done_body: Open the demo from the library and poke at it — the editor's tour will walk you through that side too. You can re-run this tour anytime from the 🎓 Tutorial button.
deeper_tut_ed_intro_body:
Two big things on screen: the preview + timeline on the left, and the side panel on the right. Everything you build for an enhancement happens here.

💡 Big idea: an enhancement turns a piece of media into something interactive — gaze, blinks, attention, or just timestamps can fire haptics, jump the playhead, loop a region, anything.
deeper_tut_ed_timeline_body:
Click or drag to seek. Each lane on the left (Rules, Regions, Haptics, Effects) holds one type of item. Press R to drop a region at the playhead, H for a haptic event.

A lane you're not using can be ignored — the count next to its name tells you what's in it.

💡 Example: an MP3 with a bass drop at 1:20 — drop a haptic event at 1:20 with a sharp pattern, then watch your viewers squirm right when it hits.
deeper_tut_ed_preview_body:
Hit Preview and the engine runs your enhancement live as the media plays — you can feel haptics and watch rules fire before you save.

No webcam? B = blink, M = mouth, A = attention-lost. The mouse stands in for gaze inside the preview.
deeper_tut_ed_metadata_body: Name, creator, description, tags — what shows up in the library tile when someone installs your file.
deeper_tut_ed_rules_body:
Each rule is a trigger → action pair. They live in this lane. Time-based triggers work on any media; gaze, blink, mouth-open, and attention-lost need video and a webcam.

Use + Rule below to add one at the playhead. Cooldowns stop a noisy trigger from firing every frame.

💡 Examples:
• Gaze on bottom-right corner → loop the current region. They have to look there to make it stop.
• Attention lost (face away from camera 10s) → seek back to the start. Stay focused, or start over.
• Region entered "Deepen" → trigger a slow-build haptic pattern. Synced reinforcement on a part of the audio.
deeper_tut_ed_selected_body: Click anything on the timeline (a region, a haptic event, a rule) and its fields land here for editing. The strip just above the editor tells you what's currently selected.
deeper_tut_ed_save_body: Validation runs on every keystroke — the strip at the bottom tells you what's broken. Save (Ctrl+S) writes to disk; the player and library hot-reload the file.
deeper_tut_ed_done_body:
That's the whole editor. You can pop this tour again from the ? button next to the title.

💡 A good first project: open the bundled demo, change a haptic pattern, save, and play it in the player to feel the difference.

Or start from scratch — pick an MP3 you love, drop haptic events on the beats, share the .ccpenh.json with anyone running CCP. They get the same experience, synced to the same audio.
desc_remote_stop_on_disconnect: Off by default: effects keep running after a controller leaves so a new controller can see the current state. Turn on to snap everything off on disconnect.
label_remote_share_avatar: Show my Discord avatar to controllers
desc_remote_share_avatar: Off by default. When on, your linked Discord avatar is shown to anyone controlling your session. Off keeps you behind a silhouette.
gate_premium_subtitle: Unlock to interact with the controls below
desc_remote_control: Hand the keys to a trusted controller. They drive your session from any phone or browser using a 6-digit code and PIN, and you choose exactly what they're allowed to trigger.
desc_remote_control_bullets:
• Light — visual triggers only
• Standard — Light + audio + overlays
• Full — everything including session control
chk_optin_directory: Show me in the Available Subjects directory

desc_takeover: Let the app run autonomous sessions on its own schedule. It picks the timing, the triggers, and the intensity — within the boundaries you set.
desc_takeover_bullets:
• Idle, random, and time-aware triggers
• Configurable intensity and cooldowns
• Choose which features it's allowed to use
guide_takeover_step1_body: Use the sliders and toggles to choose how often she acts and what she's allowed to do.
guide_takeover_step2_body: The green Start button arms her. Nothing happens until you press it - Test only works once she's running.
guide_takeover_step3_body: Within your limits she triggers effects on her own. Press Stop, or the panic key, to end it anytime.
guide_takeover_settings_body:
Interval - average time between actions.
Cooldown - minimum gap enforced after any action.
Intensity - how strong and frequent effects lean (1 gentle, 10 intense).
Announce - chance she says something before acting.
Idle / Random / Time-Aware - which moments are allowed to trigger her.
guide_takeover_tip: Tip: the X panic key instantly clears every active effect.
guide_takeover_patreon: Bambi Takeover is a Patreon perk and needs an active subscription to run.
desc_haptics: Sync your Intiface-compatible device to every event in the app — flashes, videos, bubble pops, achievements, level-ups.

tooltip_autonomy_interval: How often the Takeover checks whether to fire an action. Lower = more frequent attempts. At interval 60s she evaluates roughly once a minute, picks an enabled behavior, and decides whether to fire it based on intensity.
tooltip_autonomy_cooldown: Minimum wait between two consecutive Takeover actions. Prevents back-to-back firing even if the interval check passes. Set higher if Takeover feels too relentless.
tooltip_autonomy_intensity: Aggressiveness of the Takeover. 1 = subtle nudges (rare, soft behaviors only), 10 = relentless (frequent, overlapping, all behaviors). Scales both the fire chance per interval and which enabled behaviors are eligible.
tooltip_autonomy_announce: Chance (0-100%) that the Takeover will announce what she's about to do via a speech bubble before firing. At 0% she acts silently; at 100% every action is preceded by a callout.
tooltip_autonomy_idle: Fire more aggressively when you haven't touched mouse or keyboard for a while. Good for automated 'zone out' sessions — she ramps up as you stop fighting it.
tooltip_autonomy_random: Add randomness to the fire schedule so actions don't happen on a predictable beat. Without this, actions space out evenly along the interval; with it, the schedule jitters.
tooltip_autonomy_time_aware: Adjust intensity based on the time of day. Late night = more aggressive, daytime = gentler. Use this if you want Takeover to lean in during evening sessions.
tooltip_autonomy_flash: Let the Takeover trigger flash images from your Flash pool during random actions. Uses your normal flash settings (duration, count, filter).
tooltip_autonomy_video: Let the Takeover play mandatory videos from your Video pool. Honors your mandatory-video settings including attention checks.
tooltip_autonomy_subliminal: Let the Takeover display subliminal text overlays from your subliminal pool during random actions.
tooltip_autonomy_comment: Let her avatar say contextual comments at random moments — reinforcing phrases, trigger words, or conditioning lines even when no other action is firing.
tooltip_autonomy_bubbles: Let the Takeover spawn the bubble-pop minigame. You'll get floating bubbles you need to pop — interrupts whatever you were doing.
tooltip_autonomy_pink_filter: Let the Takeover toggle the pink screen filter on and off during random actions. Colors your whole desktop pink for immersion.
tooltip_autonomy_lock_cards: Let the Takeover force-show a Lock Card — a phrase you must type N times before the app releases control back.
tooltip_autonomy_bouncing_text: Let the Takeover display bouncing text overlays (words or phrases that drift across the screen) during random actions.
tooltip_autonomy_mind_wipe: Let the Takeover trigger the Mind Wipe effect — audio/visual static that interrupts your train of thought. Strongest Takeover behavior; use sparingly.
label_she_will_autonomously_trigger_effe: She will autonomously trigger effects based on your settings

This session is designed for your morning routine - while you work, browse, or prepare for the day. No interruptions, no demands. Just gentle whispers and soft reminders that help good girls drift into that comfortable, familiar headspace.

Features gentle subliminals like 'Good Girl', 'Bambi Sleep', and 'Giggletime' to help you start your day in a blissful, obedient haze.

You don't need to do anything special. Just... let it happen. 💗
session_gamer_girl_desc:
Time to play, Gamer Girl...

This session was designed for your gaming sessions. Keep playing, keep focusing on your game. You won't even notice what's happening in the background... at first.

Includes subtle 'Good Girl' and focus-based subliminals that won't break your concentration.

Just play your game. Let everything else happen on its own.

⚠ Set your game to Borderless Windowed mode for the full experience!

💗 Good luck, Gamer Girl...
session_gamer_girl_corner_gif_desc: Optional: Place a subtle GIF in a screen corner (great for covering minimaps!)
session_distant_doll_desc:
No need to get up, sweetheart. Stay comfortable, soft, and empty.

Filled with gentle, dreamy subliminals like 'Good Girl', 'Bambi Sleep', and 'Bimbo Doll' that wash over you.

Everything is designed to be viewed from a distance while your mind drifts away. Perfect for turning your relaxation time into a passive reprogramming session.
session_good_girls_dont_cum_desc:
A challenging denial and edging session designed to test your obedience and focus.

This session uses intense, hardcore subliminals including triggers like 'Bambi Freeze', 'Drop for Cock', and 'Cock Zombie Now'. Click the flash images if you can - but watch out, they multiply!

Your only purpose is to sit prettily and let the pink fog consume you. And remember not to touch that clitty, Good Girls Don't Cum.
session_deep_dive_desc: A longer, more immersive experience for when you have time to truly let go...
session_bambi_time_desc: Full Bambi mode. Everything turned up. Complete surrender.
session_random_drop_desc: You won't know what's coming. That's the point. Let go of control completely.

tooltip_gaze_minigame_open: Open the Gaze Minigame in a new window. Requires a working calibration.
tooltip_focus_gaze_toggle: Hands-free interaction: look at a bubble or flash for ~1s to pop it. Requires camera consent and a stored calibration.
tooltip_webcam_calibrate: Run the 16-point calibration. Stare at each target until the ring around it fills up and pulses. Re-run if you change seating or move the camera.
tooltip_webcam_tracker_test: Show the live tracker overlay so you can verify face and iris detection before starting a game.
tooltip_webcam_start_tracking: Begin webcam tracking using your saved calibration. Frames are processed locally and never leave this machine.
tooltip_webcam_review_privacy: Open the privacy info dialog: what the webcam features capture, store, and never transmit.
tooltip_webcam_show_debug_cursor: Show a small floating cursor that follows the estimated gaze point. Debug aid — useful while learning where the tracker thinks you're looking.
blink_trainer_include_videos_sub: Add video files to the swap pool
blink_trainer_consent_revoke_tooltip: Stops webcam tracking, deletes calibration, and clears your consent. You'll be re-prompted next time you enable a webcam feature.
blink_trainer_consent_revoke_confirm_bod:
Revoke webcam consent?

This will:
  • Stop webcam tracking immediately
  • Delete your calibration data
  • Disable Blink Trainer, Focus Gaze, and any webcam triggers
  • Clear your consent record

You'll be re-prompted to consent and recalibrate the next time you enable a webcam feature.

blink_trainer_quick_recal_needs_full_bod: Quick Recal only nudges an existing calibration. Run a full Calibrate first.

blink_trainer_error_no_assets: No images / GIFs found in the selected folders.
deeper_tutorial_header: Tutorial: walk me through editing my first…
deeper_tutorial_local_video_tooltip: Interactive walkthrough for editing a local video file. ~3 min.
deeper_tutorial_local_audio_tooltip: Interactive walkthrough for editing a local audio file. ~3 min.
deeper_tutorial_ht_tooltip: Interactive on-rails walkthrough — produces a finished .ccpenh.json from a sample HypnoTube TikTok URL. ~3 min.
deeper_itut_ht_step1_body: Video is selected and a working URL is pasted into Source. Click Create to open the editor.
deeper_itut_ht_step2_body: Title, creator, description and tags came from the page. Notice the lock next to Creator — that is the auto-fill lock. Click Next to continue.
deeper_itut_ht_step3_body: When the URL is HypnoTube, Creator is auto-set and locked. Click the lock to override; click again to relock. Don't click it now — just observe. Click Next to continue.
deeper_itut_ht_step4_body: The video loaded in the embedded browser. Click play to start it for a few seconds. (If the browser hasn't initialized yet, use Skip step.)
deeper_itut_ht_step5_body: Pause around 5 seconds — we'll add an effect at the playhead. Click pause.
deeper_itut_ht_step6_body: Click + Effect to drop a Haptic at the playhead. Effects are point-in-time stimuli — vibration, flash, bubbles, subliminal text, or screen overlays.
deeper_itut_ht_step7_body: The slider is at 100% by default. Drag it down to about half — anywhere between 30% and 70%. This controls how strong the haptic feels.
deeper_itut_ht_step8_body: Open the Pattern dropdown and pick any pattern (Throb, Wave, Climax, etc). The pattern shapes how the haptic ramps up and down - you'll feel it when you hit Test next.
deeper_itut_ht_step9_body: Click Test to feel the pattern on your connected haptic device. If you don't have one connected, click Skip step.
deeper_itut_ht_step10_body: Click + Rule to add a TimeReached rule at the playhead. Rules react to events (time, gaze, attention, blink) and run actions (pause, seek, fire effects).
deeper_itut_ht_step11_title: Make the rule fire at 15 seconds
deeper_itut_ht_step11_body: Type 15 in the Time (s) field. The rule will fire when the playhead crosses 15s during playback.
deeper_itut_ht_step12_body: Open the Action dropdown and pick screen_shake. A short description of each action appears below the dropdown.
deeper_itut_ht_step13_body: Type 0.7 in the Intensity field below. At playback time, when the rule fires, the screen will shake at 70% strength.
deeper_itut_ht_step14_body: You have 1 Effect and 1 Rule. Click Save to write your .ccpenh.json file to disk.
deeper_itut_ht_step15_body: The Save dialog has appeared. Use the suggested name (Title — Creator) or rename it, then click Save in the dialog. We'll wait here until the file is written.
deeper_itut_ht_step16_body: Your .ccpenh.json now contains: a Haptic effect at 5s + a TimeReached rule at 15s that triggers a screen-shake. To play it later, open the Deeper Player from the Deeper tab and load this file. The effect and rule will fire at their configured times. You can also share the .ccpenh.json with other CCP users.
deeper_itut_audio_step1_body: Audio is selected. Click Browse and choose any .mp3, .wav, .m4a, .flac or .ogg file from your computer. (If you cancel the picker, click Skip step.)
deeper_itut_audio_step2_body: Path looks good? Click Create to open the editor. If the field is still empty, click Browse first.
deeper_itut_audio_step3_body: Local files don't auto-fill metadata like HypnoTube does - you fill it in. Type a name (e.g. 'My Mix'), then click Next.
deeper_itut_audio_step4_body: The waveform replaces the video preview when you load audio. Click play to start it for a few seconds.
deeper_itut_audio_step5_body: Pause near 5s - we'll drop an effect at the playhead next. Click pause.
deeper_itut_audio_step6_body: Click + Effect to drop a Haptic at the playhead. Audio enhancements support Haptic, Bubble, Subliminal and Overlay effects (no Flash, since there's no video to flash over).
deeper_itut_audio_step7_body: Drag the slider down to about half - anywhere between 30% and 70%. This controls how strong the haptic feels on your connected device.
deeper_itut_audio_step8_body: Open the Pattern dropdown and pick any pattern. The pattern shapes how the haptic ramps up and down - you'll feel it when you hit Test next.
deeper_itut_audio_step9_body: Click Test to feel the pattern on your connected haptic device. If you don't have one, click Skip step.
deeper_itut_audio_step10_body: Click + Rule to add a TimeReached rule at the playhead. Audio rules can react to time and to regions you draw on the timeline (no gaze, blink or attention triggers - those need video).
deeper_itut_audio_step11_title: Make the rule fire at 8 seconds
deeper_itut_audio_step11_body: Type 8 in the Time (s) field. The rule will fire when the playhead crosses 8s during playback.
deeper_itut_audio_step12_body: Open the Action dropdown and pick 'Pause playback'. The audio will halt at 8s - handy for forcing the listener to dwell on a specific bar of the track.
deeper_itut_audio_step13_body: You have 1 Effect and 1 Rule. Click Save to write your .ccpenh.json file to disk.
deeper_itut_audio_step14_body: The Save dialog has appeared. Use the suggested name or rename it, then click Save in the dialog. We'll wait here until the file is written.
deeper_itut_audio_step15_body: Your .ccpenh.json now contains: a Haptic effect at 5s + a TimeReached rule at 8s that pauses playback. Open the Deeper Player from the Deeper tab to play it. You can share the file with other CCP users - anyone who has the same audio file at the bound path can play your edit.
deeper_itut_video_step1_body: Video is selected. Click Browse and choose any .mp4, .webm, .mkv, .mov, .avi or .m4v file from your computer. (If you cancel the picker, click Skip step.)
deeper_itut_video_step2_body: Path looks good? Click Create to open the editor. If the field is still empty, click Browse first.
deeper_itut_video_step3_body: Local files don't auto-fill metadata like HypnoTube does - you fill it in. Type a name (e.g. 'My Edit'), then click Next.
deeper_itut_video_step4_body: The video loaded in the embedded VLC preview. Click play to start it for a few seconds.
deeper_itut_video_step5_body: Pause near 5s - we'll drop an effect at the playhead next. Click pause.
deeper_itut_video_step6_body: Click + Effect to drop a Haptic at the playhead. Video enhancements support every effect type - Haptic, Flash, Bubble, Subliminal and Overlay.
deeper_itut_video_step7_body: Drag the slider down to about half - anywhere between 30% and 70%. This controls how strong the haptic feels on your connected device.
deeper_itut_video_step8_body: Open the Pattern dropdown and pick any pattern. The pattern shapes how the haptic ramps up and down - you'll feel it when you hit Test next.
deeper_itut_video_step9_body: Click Test to feel the pattern on your connected haptic device. If you don't have one, click Skip step.
deeper_itut_video_step10_body: Click + Rule. The default trigger is TimeReached at the playhead - but for video you can do much more. We'll change it next.
deeper_itut_video_step11_title: Switch the trigger to Attention Lost
deeper_itut_video_step11_body: Open the Trigger dropdown and pick 'Looks away (face leaves camera)'. This is video's superpower - it fires whenever the webcam tracker sees you look away. (Requires camera consent + a calibration when you actually run the enhancement.)
deeper_itut_video_step12_body: Open the Action dropdown and pick 'Shake the screen'. Look-away triggers a shake that snaps you back to attention. Cheeky.
deeper_itut_video_step13_body: Type 0.7 in the Intensity field below. When you look away, the screen will shake at 70% strength.
deeper_itut_video_step14_body: You have 1 Effect and 1 Rule. Click Save to write your .ccpenh.json file to disk.
deeper_itut_video_step15_body: The Save dialog has appeared. Use the suggested name or rename it, then click Save in the dialog. We'll wait here until the file is written.
deeper_itut_video_step16_body: Your .ccpenh.json now contains: a Haptic effect at 5s + an AttentionLost rule that screen-shakes when you look away. Open the Deeper Player from the Deeper tab to play it. Try looking away while it's running - you'll see the rule fire.
